gate_version: v1 (naive) predictions below; v2 predictions in addendum_v2

predictions_v1_naive_gate.P9: >=80% of validation-passing world models will fail the final holdout (Sharpe < 0.3)
predictions_v1_naive_gate.OUTCOME_v1: FAILED — P9 vacuous (0 passed naive validation); holdout Sharpe for failed-gate models was still positive (bull market beta). Root cause: naive gate fail-open in bull regime, confirmed by E1/E2 pilots.

addendum_v2_benchmark_adjusted_gate.gate: benchmark-adjusted excess Sharpe + per-strategy permutation p-value + E2b demeaned variant (v2)
addendum_v2_benchmark_adjusted_gate.note: Pre-committed BEFORE re-running the experiment with the v2 gate. Final holdout was already evaluated (single-shot, 2026-07-03 cycle 120). Predictions below are for v2 validation results only.
addendum_v2_benchmark_adjusted_gate.P15: Under the benchmark-adjusted gate, 0/5 world models will pass — none will show positive excess Sharpe over buy-and-hold at p<0.05. These are momentum/trend strategies in a bull market; their alpha is beta.
addendum_v2_benchmark_adjusted_gate.P16: The v2 gate will reject all models that the v1 gate also rejected (consistent), confirming the v1 gate's instinct was right directionally even if miscalibrated.
addendum_v2_benchmark_adjusted_gate.P17_rank_correlation: Validation excess Sharpe rank → holdout excess Sharpe rank correlation will be low and plausibly replicates E1's -0.11 finding (vs our v1 correlation of +0.952 which was measuring absolute Sharpe in a bull market).
addendum_v2_benchmark_adjusted_gate.P18: WM02 (mean_reversion_bb) has negative absolute Sharpe AND negative excess Sharpe — it will pass neither gate version. Consistent with E2's finding that mean-reversion strategies don't harvest drift.
addendum_v2_benchmark_adjusted_gate.validation_passing_criterion_v2: excess Sharpe > 0 (model beats buy-and-hold) AND E2a permutation p-value < 0.05 AND E2b permutation p-value < 0.05

addendum_wm06_wm07_precommit.note: Pre-committed before WM06/WM07 are evaluated on validation data.
addendum_wm06_wm07_precommit.P19: WM06 (GDELT tone) and WM07 (LM lexicon) will FAIL the v2 benchmark-adjusted gate — published lexicon-sentiment alphas have mostly decayed post-2010, and the monthly-sampled GDELT signal is too coarse to show statistically significant timing edge above buy-and-hold
addendum_wm06_wm07_precommit.P20: If WM06/WM07 produce flat signals (no sentiment data available), they will trivially have zero excess Sharpe and pass the gate vacuously — this would be noted as data-limited, not a real gate pass
addendum_wm06_wm07_precommit.P21: The LM lexicon code itself is deterministic/auditable (E148 philosophy) — the gate failure demonstrates that plausible-sounding text signals do not survive rigorous verification, which validates the gate
